perturbation.original_text: Client will be promoted as the preferred and dominant reseller of Vitamins in the Health Channel on the WebCrawler Site during the term of this Agreement. As such, Excite may not display banner advertising and/or promotional placements for any of Client's Competitors that are, in the aggregate, of equal or greater prominence and exposure than the aggregate of Client's links, advertising banners and promotional placements on the pages of the Health Channel on the WebCrawler Site during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Client will be promoted as a reseller of Vitamins in the Health Channel on the WebCrawler Site during the term of this Agreement. Excite retains the right to display banner advertising and/or promotional placements for Client's Competitors with equal or greater prominence and exposure than the aggregate of Client's links, advertising banners and promotional placements on the pages of the Health Channel on the WebCrawler Site during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated that Client will be promoted as the 'preferred and dominant reseller' and Excite may not promote competitors with equal or greater prominence. The modified version removes the 'preferred and dominant' status, giving Excite the right to promote Client's Competitors with equal or greater prominence which directly contradicts the previous statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Client will be promoted as a reseller of Vitamins in the Health Channel on the WebCrawler Site during the term of this Agreement. Excite retains the right to display banner advertising and/or promotional placements for Client's Competitors with equal or greater prominence and exposure than the aggregate of Client's links, advertising banners and promotional placements on the pages of the Health Channel on the WebCrawler Site during the term of this Agreement. For purposes of this Agreement, Client's Competitors means those merchants identified in Exhibit D attached hereto. Client may update 2 CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS OMITTED AND FILED SEPARATELY WITH THE SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION. ASTERISKS DENOTE OMISSIONS. this list in writing not more than once every six (6) months by adding other merchants whose primary business is reselling Vitamins upon the mutual agreement of Excite. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Excite may display links to Excite's own products and services anywhere in the Excite Network, and may display links to Client's Competitors in results pages of search services in response to user queries, in general directories of Web sites, in classified advertising listings and in results in the "Jango" shopping search service throughout the Excite Network. Client's preferred and dominant status as a reseller of Vitamins will be extended on the terms stated in this Section l(a) to its presence within future departments within the WebCrawler Health Channel, when launched, which may include, but are not limited to, the alternative medicine and senior living departments.
perturbation.justification: The original text ensured Client's 'preferred and dominant' position, restricting Excite from promoting competitors with equal prominence. The changed text removes this preferential status, explicitly allowing Excite to promote competitors with equal or greater prominence. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, particularly the phrase 'Client's preferred and dominant status as a reseller of Vitamins will be extended on the terms stated in this Section l(a)'. The modification creates a strong, direct contradiction regarding Client's market position and Excite's promotional rights, thus causing a potential legal issue.

perturbation.original_text: Excite guarantees to deliver the annual impressions totals set forth in Exhibit C hereto. If Excite fails to deliver the indicated number of impressions required during any annual period, Client may suspend (but not eliminate) its payments specified in Section 7 for a maximum of sixty (60) days (the "Make-Good Period) during which Excite will deliver the shortfall of such impressions.
perturbation.changed_text: Excite will attempt to deliver the annual impressions totals set forth in Exhibit C hereto. If Excite fails to deliver the indicated number of impressions required during any annual period, Client may not suspend its payments specified in Section 7. Excite is not required to deliver the shortfall of such impressions.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Excite 'guarantees' to deliver annual impressions, and if they fail, the client can suspend payments. The modified text removes the guarantee and prevents the client from suspending payments or requiring that Excite is required to deliver the shorfall of impressions, creating a contradiction. This could lead to disagreements over fulfillment and payment obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Excite will attempt to deliver the annual impressions totals set forth in Exhibit C hereto. If Excite fails to deliver the indicated number of impressions required during any annual period, Client may not suspend its payments specified in Section 7. Excite is not required to deliver the shortfall of such impressions. The parties agree to cooperate in good faith to evaluate the quality and performance of the placements used to deliver the impressions during the Make-Good Period. Until such shortfall is delivered, no impressions will be deemed delivered for the next annual period. If Excite has not achieved the required annual impression delivery by the end of the Make-Good Period, Client may then terminate this Agreement upon written notice within ten (10) days following the end of the Make-Good Period. Client's termination of the Agreement in accordance with the previous sentence will not relieve Excite of its obligation to deliver any previously paid for but undelivered impressions. If Excite achieves the annual impression delivery goal at any time during the Make-Good Period, the term of this Agreement will continue and Client shall immediately resume payment of the sponsorship and advertising fees specified in Section 7.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text are essentially the same. The changed text, however, introduces a direct contradiction. The original text 'guarantees' delivery and allows suspension of payment if delivery fails. The changed text removes the guarantee, prevents suspension of payments, and removes the requirement to deliver the shortfall, which contradicts the original obligation and the implications of the contradicted section. The contradicted section states, "Excite will attempt to deliver the annual impressions totals set forth in Exhibit C hereto. If Excite fails to deliver the indicated number of impressions required during any annual period, Client may not suspend its payments specified in Section 7. Excite is not required to deliver the shortfall of such impressions... Until such shortfall is delivered, no impressions will be deemed delivered for the next annual period." This directly conflicts with the original agreement to guarantee delivery and make good on shortfalls, rendering the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable, a clear in-text contradiction as per the definition of Structural Flaws.

perturbation.original_text: User Data will be owned by Client, and subject to the limitations contained herein, Client grants to Excite a non-exclusive license to use the User Data for the purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: User Data will be owned by Excite, and subject to the limitations contained herein, Excite grants to Client a non-exclusive license to use the User Data for the purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that 'User Data will be owned by Client', while the modified version inverts this statement to read that 'User Data will be owned by Excite'. This direct contradiction creates ambiguity regarding the rightful owner of user data, which is a fundamental aspect of the agreement, and could cause significant legal disputes regarding privacy and data usage rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: User Data will be owned by Excite, and subject to the limitations contained herein, Excite grants to Client a non-exclusive license to use the User Data for the purposes of this Agreement. h) In order to facilitate optimization of Client's sponsorship program, Client will make good faith efforts to develop tracking and reporting capabilities to correlate information regarding transaction activity by users referred to the Client Site from the Excite Network to the various promotional placements and advertising banners displayed on the Excite Network. Client will provide to Excite all User Data and 11 12 CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS OMITTED AND FILED SEPARATELY WITH THE SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION. ASTERISKS DENOTE OMISSIONS. user transaction reports collected by Client within thirty (30) days following the end of each calendar month during the term of this Agreement in a mutually-determined electronic format.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text regarding the ownership of User Data. The changed text states 'User Data will be owned by Excite,' while the contradicted text includes the same statement. This creates a clear in-text contradiction and ambiguity about which entity, Excite or Client, owns the User Data, which falls under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
